site_title: Tutela Digital® | Legal Infrastructure for Digital Evidence

home.pageTitle: Digital Evidence Preservation with Legal Validity
home.trustItem1Desc: Structured technical documentation before litigation, ensuring evidence integrity from its origin.
home.trustItem2Desc: Integration with e-Notariado for onboarding with notarial seal, guaranteeing recognized documentary authenticity.
home.trustItem3Desc: Cryptographic technical mechanisms that guarantee the immutability and traceability of each preserved asset.
home.trustItem4Desc: Structured to meet CPC, LGPD and evidentiary admissibility standard requirements.
home.pillarsSubtitle: Technical and legal components that support evidentiary preservation with rigor and reliability.
home.legalP2: Digital evidence constitutes a legitimate means of demonstrating facts in judicial, administrative or arbitral proceedings, as provided in art. 369 of the CPC and recognized by consolidated jurisprudence — provided it is preserved with verifiable technical integrity and documented chain of custody.
home.legalLink2: Chain of custody of digital evidence
home.home_trust_p1: Tutela Digital® provides structured digital evidentiary preservation infrastructure for public organizations, companies and individuals who require technical documentation of digital assets with verifiable integrity and auditable traceability.
home.home_trust_p2: The structured preservation of digital assets is an essential step for producing evidence in judicial, administrative or arbitral proceedings. Tutela Digital® operates primarily in the pre-litigation phase, technically organizing digital evidence with verifiable integrity, chain of custody documentation and support for notarial formalization via e-Notariado.
home.home_verticals_gov_desc: Evidentiary custody of public acts, administrative contracts and sensitive documents with transparency, regulatory compliance and auditable traceability.
home.home_verticals_corp_desc: Protection of strategic documents, intellectual property, regulatory compliance and governance of corporate digital assets with legal validity.
home.home_verticals_personal_desc: Digital asset protection, succession planning, custody of personal assets and document confidentiality with notarial seal.
home.home_pillars_preservation_desc: Technical registration and preservation of digital assets with formal documentation of integrity, authenticity and origin, structured for evidentiary support.
home.home_pillars_integrity_desc: High-standard cryptographic algorithms that produce mathematical evidence of immutability, enabling independent verification by any authority.
home.home_pillars_custody_desc: Immutable and auditable history of all operations performed on each digital asset, with temporal and authorial identification of each event.
home.home_pillars_admissibility_desc: Infrastructure structured to support the admissibility analysis of digital evidence before courts, administrative tribunals and arbitral chambers.
home.home_applicability_title: Digital Evidence and the Brazilian Legal System
home.home_applicability_desc: Digital evidentiary preservation can be used for judicial proceedings, administrative defenses, arbitral procedures, internal investigations and notarial formalizations, subject to assessment by the competent authority.
home.home_cta_title: Protect your digital assets with legal validity
home.home_cta_desc: Access the platform and start the structured preservation of your digital assets with auditable chain of custody and notarial seal.
home.hero_subtitle: Technical and legal infrastructure for structured digital evidence preservation, with verifiable integrity, auditable chain of custody and support for admissibility in judicial, administrative and arbitral proceedings.

preservation.title: How to Preserve Digital Evidence with Integrity and Documented Chain of Custody
preservation.p1: Preserving digital evidence requires technical mechanisms capable of chronologically and structurally recording events related to the asset.
preservation.p2: The digital chain of custody documents deposit, identifier generation and timestamp registration.
preservation.p3: Technical organization contributes to greater documentary clarity in proceedings.

legalBasis.title: Legal Basis of Digital Evidence Preservation under Brazilian Law
legalBasis.p1: Brazilian law admits documentary evidence produced electronically provided its integrity and authenticity are demonstrated.
legalBasis.p2: Structured digital records may support the formation of judicial conviction.
legalBasis.p3: Formalization through notarial deed may reinforce chronological documentation.
legalBasis.h2Main: Legal Basis for Electronic Evidence Admissibility
legalBasis.h3CivilProcedure: Code of Civil Procedure — Arts. 369, 422 and 439

security.metaDesc: Security infrastructure with end-to-end encryption, authentication with notarial seal via e-Notariado, immutable technical registration and verifiable chain of custody.
security.title: Integrity Architecture Applied to Evidentiary Preservation
security.p1: Technical and legal foundations that underpin the digital evidentiary preservation infrastructure with verifiable rigor.
security.p2: Tutela Digital®'s infrastructure was developed to provide technical mechanisms for integrity preservation and traceability combined with legal documentation. Each system component was structured considering information security requirements, regulatory compliance and evidentiary recognition before competent authorities.
security.p3: Integration with the e-Notariado system ensures the onboarding process is carried out with notarial seal, while structured cryptographic mechanisms ensure the integrity and traceability of assets throughout the entire preservation cycle — from deposit to eventual use as evidence.
security.pillarsSubtitle: Six complementary layers ensuring technical integrity, access control and evidentiary validity.
security.h3AccessControl: Exclusive Access Control by the Holder
security.eNotarialDesc: Onboarding with identity validation via the official Brazilian notary platform, conferring notarial seal on the custody process and guaranteeing non-repudiation regarding the holder's identity.
security.encryptionDesc: High-standard cryptographic algorithms protect assets in transit and at rest, preventing unauthorized access and ensuring confidentiality throughout the entire preservation cycle.
security.blockchainDesc: Distributed and temporally verifiable registration that guarantees the integrity and traceability of all operations, functioning as complementary evidence of the asset's existence and immutability.
security.nonRepudiationDesc: Combined technical and legal mechanisms that prevent the denial of authorship or allegation of subsequent alteration of the custodied assets, sustaining evidentiary validity.
security.chainOfCustodyDesc: Complete, chronological and unalterable history of all actions performed on each digital asset, with agent identification, verifiable timestamp and nature of each operation.
security.evidentialValidityDesc: Infrastructure structured to support the admissibility of preserved assets as evidence in judicial, administrative and arbitral proceedings, subject to assessment by the competent authority.
security.reliabilityTitle: Evidentiary Reliability as a Design Principle
security.reliabilityDesc: The legal reliability of digital evidence depends on the technical demonstration of integrity, traceability and authorship control. Tutela Digital®'s architecture was structured from these requirements — not as an additional feature, but as the infrastructure's foundation.
security.reliabilityDesc2: Each design decision meets criteria derived from art. 369 of the CPC, information security standards and admissibility requirements recognized by the jurisprudence of superior courts.
security.check1: Integrity verifiable by any authority, without platform dependency
security.check2: Authenticity attested by notarial seal via e-Notariado
security.check3: Auditable and chronologically documented chain of custody
security.check4: Immutable and independently verifiable temporal registration
security.check5: Exclusive holder control with non-repudiation mechanisms
security.check6: Compliance with LGPD, CPC and information security standards
security.ctaDesc: Access the platform and start the custody of your digital assets with verifiable integrity and notarial seal.

howItWorks.pageTitle: How Evidentiary Preservation Works | Tutela Digital®
howItWorks.metaDesc: Technical process for digital evidentiary preservation: registration, notarial onboarding via e-Notariado, deposit and registration with auditable chain of custody.
howItWorks.title: How Evidentiary Preservation Works
howItWorks.subtitle: A four-step structured process for digital asset evidentiary preservation with verifiable registration, notarial seal and auditable chain of custody.
howItWorks.processDescription: Tutela Digital® has developed a process that combines the ease of use of a modern digital platform with the technical-legal rigor required to guarantee the evidentiary validity of custody. In four steps, you start protecting your digital assets with notarial seal, verifiable integrity and auditable chain of custody.
howItWorks.processDescription2: To understand the application of this logic in asset organization and protection, see
howItWorks.stepsSubtitle: From account creation to formal registration with notarial seal, each step is structured to ensure evidence integrity and admissibility.
howItWorks.stepPlatformRegistrationDesc: Access the platform and complete your initial registration with basic identification information. The process is intuitive and can be started in minutes — no software installation or specialized technical knowledge required.
howItWorks.step1Note: Data protected by encryption from the first access
howItWorks.stepPlanSelectionDesc: Select the plan appropriate to your custody needs, asset volume and storage capacity. Plans are structured to serve individuals, companies and public organizations, with different capacity levels and features.
howItWorks.step2Note: All plans include chain of custody and technical registration
howItWorks.stepNotaryInterviewDesc: Schedule and conduct the interview with the notary via the official e-Notariado platform. This step confers notarial seal on the onboarding process, legally binding the holder to the custody account and ensuring non-repudiation regarding the identity of the asset holder.
howItWorks.step3Note: Legal requirement for custody with full evidentiary validity
howItWorks.stepDepositAndRecordDesc: The digital asset undergoes technical integrity preservation mechanisms — including cryptographic hash, immutable temporal registration and chain of custody documentation — formally initiating the evidentiary preservation cycle with verifiable traceability.
howItWorks.step4Note: From this point, any alteration to the asset is technically detectable
howItWorks.legalValidityDesc: The admissibility of digital evidence is a matter subject to the assessment of the competent authority — whether the court, arbitrator or administrative authority. Tutela Digital®'s infrastructure operates in the structured preservation and technical documentation of the asset, creating the conditions for the evidence to be evaluated with maximum credibility.
howItWorks.legalValidityDesc2: Pursuant to art. 369 of the CPC, parties have the right to employ all legal and morally legitimate means to prove the truth of the facts. Structured technical preservation is the first step in ensuring that digital evidence withstands the adversarial process.
howItWorks.infoItem2: Defenses in administrative and sanctioning procedures
howItWorks.relatedTitle: Digital Assets — Protection and Custodial Asset Management
howItWorks.ctaDesc: Access the platform and begin the process of preserving your digital assets with notarial seal, verifiable integrity and auditable chain of custody.

government.heroTitle: Digital assets in the public sector: existence is not enough — control, traceability and evidentiary integrity are essential
government.heroSubtitle: Documents, evidence, contracts and digital records are public assets. Without governance structure, they lose traceability, integrity and evidentiary value. Tutela Digital® delivers the technical and legal layer required by public administration.
government.introTitle: The State's digital assets require structure
government.introP1: Digital assets in the public sector range from electronic administrative contracts and disciplinary evidence to confidential databases and public policy records.
government.introP2: Without governance, these assets may legally exist but become unusable — undermining transparency, accountability and legal certainty.
government.asideContent: Article 37 of the Brazilian Federal Constitution establishes the principles of legality, impersonality, morality, publicity and efficiency.
government.normativeTitle: What the law requires for digital asset management
government.normativeCard1Desc: Requires active and passive transparency, with accessible and auditable records. Lack of traceability may constitute an administrative violation.
government.normativeCard2Desc: Requires control of personal data with records of operations, security, and proof of consent and deletion. Public administration follows specific provisions (arts. 23–32) under ANPD supervision.
government.normativeCard3Desc: Lack of integrity and traceability in digital assets may constitute an administrative irregularity and, where specific intent is proven (art. 1, §1 of Law 14.230/2021), qualify as impropriety under art. 11.
government.normativeCard4Desc: Establishes requirements for preserving official digital documents, including hashes, timestamps, and chain of custody, ensuring equivalence to the original physical document.
government.desafioTitle: Where the risk lies for public administration
government.desafioLead: Lack of governance transforms public records into risk elements: loss of control, evidentiary fragility and regulatory exposure.
government.sem1: Access lost or contested in proceedings
government.sem2: Violation of the Access to Information Law
government.sem3Alt: Administrative improbity due to lack of traceability
government.com2: Compliance with LGPD and data processing records
government.com3: Chain of custody aligned with evidentiary standards
government.whatChangesLead: Tutela Digital® transforms digital records into controllable assets with verifiable integrity.
government.mechanismsTitle: Compliance with ICP-Brasil and international standards
government.mechanismsDesc: The platform uses qualified electronic signatures (ICP-Brasil), SHA-256 hash, RFC 3161 timestamps, and immutable logs. All records meet the recommended technical requirements for digital evidence in courts and control bodies, according to CPC, arts. 422 and 439, and Decree 10.278/2020. Admissibility is assessed by the competent court.
government.useCasesLead: Tutela Digital® ensures integrity and legal certainty.
government.useCase1Desc: custody of digital evidence with an unquestionable chain of custody.
government.useCase2Desc: storage with verifiable integrity and traceability for control by Courts of Accounts.
government.useCase3Desc: management of LAI requests with immutable records and proof of response.
government.useCase4Desc: registration of processing operations, proof of legal bases, and secure data deletion.
government.useCaseDisclaimer: Legal compliance depends on proper implementation and use of the platform by the contracting entity. Admissibility of digital evidence is a procedural matter assessed by the competent court.
government.impactBar1Desc: Automated LAI compliance with auditable public records
government.impactBar2Desc: Records of operations and compliance with the LGPD regime for public administration (arts. 23–32)
government.impactBar3Desc: Evidence meeting ICP-Brasil, SHA-256, and RFC 3161 technical standards
government.auditSeloDesc: Compatible with TCU, TCEs and CGU · Logs with chain of custody

companies.heroTitle:
Corporate digital assets:
existing on a server is not the same as existing in law
companies.heroSubtitle: Electronic contracts, emails, decision records, intellectual property and corporate evidence only have legal value when preserved with integrity, traceability and a verifiable chain of custody. Tutela Digital® provides this structure.
companies.heroBadge3: CPC · Consumer Code · Civil Internet Framework
companies.introTitle: The company's digital assets require legal structure
companies.introP1: In the corporate environment, digital assets include electronic contracts, internal and external communications, corporate decision records, compliance documents, digital intellectual property and evidentiary records in labor and civil disputes. These are assets that can represent million-dollar obligations, decisive evidence or hidden liabilities — depending on how they are custodied.
companies.introP2: The most common mistake companies make is confusing storage with custody. A file saved on the server exists operationally, but may not exist legally: without a verifiable hash, timestamp, access log and chain of custody, the adverse party can challenge the document's authenticity with relative ease. Tutela Digital® bridges this gap.
companies.asideContent: Article 422 of the Brazilian Code of Civil Procedure requires that electronic documents presented in court be converted into the form established by law and that their authenticity can be verified. Article 439 determines that the judge will assess the evidentiary weight of the document based on the accompanying elements. Without verifiable integrity and chain of custody, the company runs the concrete risk of having its digital evidence disallowed or disregarded.
companies.normativeTitle: What the law requires for corporate digital asset management
companies.normativeCard1Desc: Requires controllers to maintain records of processing operations (art. 37), adopt technical and administrative security measures (art. 46) and be able to prove compliance before the ANPD. The absence of auditable records may constitute an offense subject to a fine of up to 2% of revenue, capped at R$ 50 million per infraction (art. 52).
companies.normativeCard2Desc: Articles 422 and 439 govern the admissibility and evidentiary value of electronic documents in court. Documents without verifiable integrity (hash, timestamp) can be challenged by the opposing party on grounds of lack of authenticity. Poorly custodied digital evidence is weak evidence.
companies.normativeCard3Desc: Article 13 requires connection providers to keep connection logs for 1 year. Article 15 requires the storage of application logs for 6 months. Companies operating digital platforms or online services are liable for the storage and integrity of these logs and may face civil and criminal liability for non-compliance (art. 12).
companies.normativeCard4Desc: Article 43 guarantees the consumer the right to access data stored about them. Companies that process customer data must prove the legal basis for each operation and demonstrate storage security. The absence of auditable records exposes the company to claims for moral and material damages arising from inadequate processing of personal data.
companies.contrasteTitle: Where the risk lies for the company
companies.contrasteLead: Lack of governance of digital assets transforms corporate documents and evidence into liabilities: challengeable evidence, regulatory sanctions and exposure to fraud that is difficult to contest.
companies.without1: Electronic contracts challenged due to lack of verifiable integrity
companies.without2: LGPD fines of up to 2% of revenue due to inability to prove compliance
companies.without3: Digital evidence disallowed in labor, civil and arbitration disputes due to lack of authenticity
companies.without4: Internal document fraud without auditable trail — risk of joint liability of directors
companies.with1: Contracts with SHA-256 hash and RFC 3161 timestamp — verifiable authenticity in court
companies.with2: Auditable records of processing operations — proof of LGPD compliance before the ANPD
companies.with3: Digital evidence with chain of custody for labor, civil and arbitration disputes, with technical compliance with the requirements of arts. 422 and 439 of the CPC
companies.with4: Immutable access and modification logs — full traceability that isolates individual liability
companies.whatChangesTitle: What changes with legal custody of digital assets
companies.whatChangesLead: Tutela Digital® transforms the company's documentary infrastructure into a defensible legal asset — with every document verifiable, every access tracked, and every piece of evidence ready to be presented.
companies.check1: Verifiable integrity via SHA-256 cryptographic hash — any subsequent alteration is detectable
companies.check2: RFC 3161 timestamp with ICP-Brasil certification authority — legally binding date and time
companies.check3: Immutable access logs — traceability of who accessed, modified or deleted each document
companies.check4: Continuous chain of custody from creation to presentation in court or arbitration — compliance with CPC, arts. 422 and 439
companies.check5: Records of personal data processing operations — proof of LGPD compliance during ANPD inspections
companies.mechanismsTitle: Compliance with ICP-Brasil and international standards
companies.mechanismsDesc: The platform operates with qualified electronic signatures (ICP-Brasil), SHA-256 hash, RFC 3161 timestamps and immutable logs with version control. Each document receives a unique identifier that allows independent verification of authenticity — without relying on the company's word or third parties.
companies.mechanismsDesc2: All records meet the recommended technical requirements for digital evidence production under CPC, arts. 422 and 439. Admissibility is assessed by the competent court on a case-by-case basis.
companies.useCasesTitle: Direct applications in the corporate environment
companies.useCasesLead: Tutela Digital® is applied in contexts that combine high legal risk with high document volume.
companies.useCase1Desc: custody of emails, messages, contracts and communications with chain of custody — counter‑evidence against allegations of harassment, irregular dismissal or breach of obligations.
companies.useCase2Desc: preservation of proposals, electronic acceptances, amendments and terminations with verifiable integrity — eliminates challenges based on alteration.
companies.useCase3Desc: structured documentation of approval processes, corporate decision records and internal control evidence for audits and M&A.
companies.useCase4Desc: registration of creations, projects and trade secrets with verifiable date and authorship — basis for actions of unfair competition and NDA breach.
companies.useCase5Desc: delivery of digital evidence with full technical compliance — arbitral chambers such as ICC, CAM-CCBC and FGV have been demanding increasingly strict standards for document authentication.
companies.useCaseDisclaimer: Legal compliance depends on proper implementation and use of the platform by the contracting company, as per technical guidelines provided by Tutela Digital®. Admissibility of digital evidence is a procedural matter assessed by the competent court.
companies.impactBar1Desc: Verifiable authenticity and date independently — eliminates the main allegation of challenge
companies.impactBar2Desc: Auditable records of processing operations for ANPD inspections and consumer lawsuits
companies.impactBar3Desc: Immutable access logs — isolates responsibilities and prevents internal document fraud
companies.impactBar4Desc: Structured documentation speeds up audits and reduces contingencies in M&A processes
companies.arbitrationSeloTitle: Compatible with national and international arbitral chambers
companies.arbitrationSeloDesc: ICC · CAM-CCBC · FGV · CAMARB · CCI · CIESP · Brazil-Canada Chamber of Commerce

individuals.heroTitle:
You take care of what you have in life.
But what happens to it afterwards?
individuals.heroSubtitle: Cryptocurrencies, NFTs, accounts in international exchanges, social media profiles, unpublished music, unpublished books — these are real assets with economic, cultural and sentimental value. Without custody and planning, they can disappear, be disputed or become forever inaccessible. Tutela Digital® preserves this legacy while you can still decide its fate.
individuals.heroBadge3: Civil Code · Copyright Law · Cryptoassets Act
individuals.introTitle: Digital assets exist. The law governing them is still not clear.
individuals.introP1: The proposed reform of the Brazilian Civil Code (Bill 4/2025, pending in the Senate since January 2025) defines digital assets as "the set of intangible and immaterial assets, with economic, personal or cultural value, existing in digital format." This definition explicitly recognizes that cryptoassets, social media profiles, unpublished works, photo collections and accounts on financial platforms are assets — transferable, protectable and planable.
individuals.introP2: The problem is that the 2002 Civil Code has not yet been reformed, and current case law, in the absence of specific legislation, tends to treat digital assets as non-transferable upon death. This means that today, without active and documented planning, a person's digital assets may simply disappear. Tutela Digital® fills this gap: it preserves, documents and structures access to these assets so that their destination can be proven and fulfilled.
individuals.asideContent: Flávio Tartuce, the rapporteur of the jurists' commission that drafted the Civil Code reform, stated that "today we have nothing on this topic in legislation" and that the lack of regulation leads the judiciary to conclude that "when we die, these rights die with us." Bill 4/2025 seeks to change this — but until it is approved, the 2002 Civil Code (Law 10.406/2002) remains in force, with no express provision for digital inheritance.
individuals.dimensionsTitle: Three dimensions of personal digital assets
individuals.dimensionsLead: Personal digital assets are not homogeneous. They are divided into three dimensions with distinct legal natures, risks and custody needs.
individuals.dim1Desc: Cryptocurrencies, NFTs, tokens, balances in exchanges and accounts in international brokers (offshore). These are assets with measurable economic value, subject to regulation under Law 14.478/2022 and Central Bank Resolutions 519–521/2025, and subject to inheritance tax in probate. The central risk: without private keys and access documentation, these assets become unrecoverable — it is estimated that 7.8 million bitcoins are permanently inaccessible due to lost credentials.
individuals.dim2Desc: Professional profiles (LinkedIn), social media accounts with built audiences (Instagram, YouTube, X), communities and digital history. Platforms like Facebook and Instagram allow the account holder to designate a "legacy contact" or request memorialization — but they require proof of death and, in many cases, prior instruction by the holder. Without planning, accounts are closed or left in indefinite limbo, losing the value of the built legacy.
individuals.dim3Desc: Digital manuscripts, unpublished music, photographs, scripts, compositions, artistic projects and any intellectual creation in digital format. The Copyright Law (Law 9.610/1998) and the Brazilian Constitution (art. 5, XXVII) guarantee that copyrights are transferable to heirs for 70 years after the author's death. Without custody, these works exist only on vulnerable devices — subject to loss, corruption or claims of inauthenticity.
individuals.dim4Tag: Dimension 4 — Protection of rights during life
individuals.dim4Desc: Harassment conversations, evidence of online defamation, records of breached agreements, privacy violations and evidence for labor, family or consumer disputes. The Civil Procedure Code (art. 369) guarantees the right to evidence by any lawful means; art. 422 requires verifiable authenticity for electronic documents. Without custody, real evidence can be disregarded.
individuals.whatChangesTitle: Custody that preserves the present and prepares the future
individuals.whatChangesLead: Tutela Digital® operates on two simultaneous time horizons: it preserves evidence for immediate use in any situation requiring digital proof, and it structures the record of digital assets so that they can be found, identified and transmitted when needed.
individuals.check1: Recording of digital assets with SHA-256 hash and RFC 3161 timestamp — independently verifiable authorship, date and content
individuals.check2: Documented chain of custody for unpublished works — basis for exercising copyright (Copyright Law, art. 11) and proving prior creation
individuals.check3: Preservation of digital evidence independent of the originating platform — immune to later deletion by the platform holder
individuals.check4: Exclusive control by the holder — you decide who can access each asset, under what conditions and when
individuals.check5: Record structure prepared for integration with future digital succession mechanisms — including death verification and conditional access activation
individuals.asideTechTitle: The future: your assets custodied today, transmitted according to your will
individuals.asideTechP1: Tutela Digital® is developing a digital succession planning layer that will allow the holder to instruct, today, the destination of each custodied asset — and ensure that this instruction is fulfilled at the right time, automatically and legally documented, without relying on the memory or action of third parties.
individuals.asideTechP2: All within the legal framework of Bill 4/2025 and the possibility of a digital will already provided for in the Civil Code (arts. 1,857 and 1,876), which allow the testator to freely dispose of their assets and post-mortem instructions.
individuals.useCasesTitle: Situations where custody and digital inheritance make a difference
individuals.useCasesLead: From protecting rights in the present to planning your legacy for the future.
individuals.useCase1Desc: registration and custody of wallets, exchanges and balances in national and international brokers — documents the existence and value of assets for probate and inheritance tax purposes, and preserves access for designated heirs (Civil Code, art. 1,784; Law 14.478/2022).
individuals.useCase2Desc: prior documented instruction on the fate of profiles on LinkedIn, Instagram, YouTube and other platforms — memorialization, transfer to designated heir or orderly closure, according to each platform's policy and the holder's will. The documentation preserved by Tutela Digital® serves as proof of the original instruction before platforms and the estate.
individuals.useCase3Desc: custody of manuscripts, music, compositions, photographs, scripts and unpublished digital creations with verifiable date and authorship — protects the author's moral and economic rights (Copyright Law, arts. 24 and 28) and ensures that the collection is not lost upon death or failure of the storage devices.
individuals.useCase4Desc: preservation of messages, screenshots and communications with verifiable date and authorship — basis for labor complaints, police reports and civil lawsuits for moral damages (Civil Code, arts. 186 and 927; Criminal Code, arts. 138 to 140).
individuals.useCase5Desc: preservation of conversations, agreements, quotes and communications with verifiable integrity — strengthens position in consumer protection agencies, Small Claims Courts, family courts and child custody and support proceedings.
individuals.useCase6Desc: recording of incidents and communications with companies — basis for exercising rights before the ANPD (LGPD, art. 42) and lawsuits based on the Child and Adolescent Statute (Law 8.069/1990) and Law 14.811/2024 for the protection of minors.
individuals.useCaseDisclaimer: Legal compliance depends on the correct use of the platform by the user, in accordance with Tutela Digital® technical guidelines. The admissibility of evidence and the transferability of digital assets are matters assessed by the competent court on a case‑by‑case basis, varying according to the type of proceeding, the asset involved and the applicable law at the time.
individuals.impactBar1Desc: Cryptoassets, NFTs and offshore accounts documented for probate and transmission
individuals.impactBar2Desc: Documented instruction for LinkedIn, Instagram, YouTube and other digital profiles
individuals.impactBar3Desc: Music, manuscripts, photographs and creations with verifiable authorship and date (Copyright Law)
individuals.impactBar4Desc: Proof of harassment, defamation, breach of agreements and privacy violations
individuals.futureSeloTitle: In development: automated transmission upon death
individuals.futureSeloDesc: The platform is building the infrastructure to ensure that the holder's will regarding their digital assets is automatically fulfilled at the right time, without relying on third parties.
individuals.legalTitle: The law that exists, the one that is coming, and what to do in the meantime
individuals.legalLead: Brazilian law is in transition regarding digital inheritance. Understanding the current state of the law is the first step to protecting your digital assets now.
individuals.legalCard1Title: Civil Code — will and disposition of assets
individuals.legalCard1Desc: A private will (art. 1,876) allows the holder to instruct the fate of their assets in writing or by mechanical process. Article 1,857 guarantees broad freedom to dispose of up to 100% of available assets. These are the current instruments for planning digital inheritance — while the Civil Code reform is pending.
individuals.legalCard2Desc: Proposes to define "digital assets" as the set of assets with economic, personal or cultural value in digital format, integrating them into the estate. It prohibits heirs from accessing private messages (post‑mortem privacy), but ensures transmission of assets with appreciable economic value. Under review by a Senate committee with a vote expected in 2026.
individuals.legalCard3Desc: The Brazilian Constitution guarantees the transferability of copyright to heirs for the term fixed by law. The Copyright Law (Law 9.610/1998) sets this term at 70 years after the author's death (art. 41). Unpublished digital creations — music, texts, images — generate rights from creation, regardless of registration. Custody with a verifiable date is proof of prior existence and authorship.
individuals.legalCard4Desc: Law 14.478/2022 and Central Bank Resolutions 519–521/2025 (effective from 2026) establish the regulatory framework for virtual assets in Brazil. Cryptoassets are assets with economic value subject to taxation (RFB Normative Instruction 1.888/2019) and probate. Documenting existence and ownership is a condition for heirs to legally access them — without it, the assets may remain indefinitely inaccessible.
individuals.ctaTitle:
Your digital assets exist today.
Ensure they exist tomorrow — and for whomever you choose.
individuals.ctaDesc: Cryptoassets, unpublished works, profiles built over years, lifelong collections — all of this can vanish without a trace due to lack of custody and planning. Tutela Digital® preserves these assets now and is building the infrastructure to ensure your will regarding them is fulfilled when needed.
individuals.ctaItem1: Immediate custody of digital assets with verifiable cryptographic integrity
individuals.ctaItem2: Registration of unpublished works with verifiable date and authorship — copyright protection from creation
individuals.ctaItem3: Documented instruction on the fate of profiles, accounts and assets for designated heirs
individuals.ctaItem4: Structure prepared for integration with death verification and future conditional activation

hero.cta: Understand how to structure the protection of your digital assets

problem.title: The Legal Problem
problem.text: The increasing digitization of assets has created a new class of property — digital assets — whose existence is often not reflected in traditional legal structures. The absence of inventory, custody, and proper documentation may compromise access, transfer, and evidentiary use.

scale.title: The Scale of the Problem
items: 12 million individuals in Brazil holding crypto assets
Only 12% of companies have formal custody policies
60 million individuals exposed to digital assets

taxonomy.title: What Are Digital Assets (Legal Perspective)
items: Non-fungible tokens (NFTs) as intangible movable assets
taxonomy.note: Loss of credentials may compromise access to the asset itself.

regulatory.title: Regulatory Gaps
items: No consolidated legal framework for digital inventory
Limited institutional standards for digital custody

succession.title: Digital Succession
succession.text: Lack of structured guidance may lead to loss of access, difficulties in estate procedures, and potential professional liability.

infrastructure.title: Legal-Digital Infrastructure
infrastructure.text: Proper digital asset management requires identification of the holder, formal expression of intent, cryptographic evidence, verifiable integrity, and an auditable trail.

implementation.title: How This Structure Is Implemented
items: Formal consent recorded via digital notarization

context.title: Regulatory Context
context.text: Global regulatory convergence (e.g., MiCA in the EU) indicates increasing requirements for governance of digital assets.

ctaFinal.text: Structuring digital asset governance is no longer optional.
ctaFinal.cta: Learn how to implement this approach in practice

diagnostic.metaTitle: Digital Asset Risk Assessment | Tutela Digital®
diagnostic.metaDescription: Discover your risk level in digital assets. Evaluate governance, access, traceability and identify legal and operational vulnerabilities.
diagnostic.heroTitle:
What is the risk level
of your digital assets?
diagnostic.heroSub: Evaluate governance, access and traceability in less than 2 minutes and identify vulnerabilities before they become irreversible.
diagnostic.q1Text: Do you have a structured inventory of your digital assets?
diagnostic.q1Hint: Consider any form of mapping — spreadsheet, document or system.
diagnostic.q1Opt0: Yes, I have a complete inventory
diagnostic.q2Text: Is there access control and recovery for these assets?
diagnostic.q2Hint: Documented credentials, contingency protocols or securely shared access.
diagnostic.q2Opt1: Partial — depends on a single person
diagnostic.q3Text: Do your assets have traceability or verifiable history?
diagnostic.q3Hint: Audit trail, event logs, timestamp or cryptographic integrity.
diagnostic.step4Title: Almost there — provide your details to receive the result
diagnostic.step4Hint: Your data is used exclusively to send the diagnosis. No data is shared with third parties.
diagnostic.postDesc: Based on the result, you identify critical points and understand which measures guarantee control, access and validity of your assets.
diagnostic.postLink: Learn how to structure your digital assets
diagnostic.resultCtaLow: See how to maintain the structure

low.title: Low risk
low.description: Your digital assets have a good governance, access and traceability structure. Periodic review is recommended to maintain maturity.
low.recommendation: Keep your inventory updated and review access controls annually.

moderate.title: Moderate risk
moderate.description: There are gaps in at least one of the three evaluated dimensions (inventory, access or traceability). Vulnerabilities may become critical in the short term.
moderate.recommendation: We suggest implementing a structured inventory and documenting access and recovery protocols.

high.title: High risk
high.description: Significant weaknesses in governance, access or traceability. The likelihood of loss, dispute or evidentiary inadmissibility is high.
high.recommendation: We recommend a detailed technical assessment and implementation of integrity and chain of custody controls.

critical.title: Critical risk
critical.description: Absence of minimum governance structure. Assets may be considered legally non-existent or unrecoverable.
critical.recommendation: Urgent intervention required. Consult digital asset governance specialists.
